metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the concrete re-drive bound (e.g. `max_redrives: int = 5`) and the exhaustion action (e.g. drop wipChain, log WARN, reset to `selecting`) directly in the AC.
findings.finding: 'Bounded re-drive attempts' is an unspecified bound — a real design choice left entirely to the executor.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — FFO TOCTOU guard + re-drive bullet: 'looping (bounded re-drive attempts)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the TOCTOU bullet: 'bounded re-drive attempts (default: 5); on exhaustion, abandon the wipChain: reset phase to `idle`, log a structured WARNING with `{change_id, attempts}`, and allow the selector to re-queue the change on the next cycle.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the `wipChain` state transition after a successful submit (e.g., 'on `POST /submit` success: set `phase = idle`, clear `tested_sha` and `chain_member_ids`, then begin next FIFO selection query') so the loop's terminal-to-start transition is unambiguous.
findings.finding: The `wipChain` schema (AC5) specifies `phase ∈ {selecting, rebasing, awaiting_verified, submitting, idle}` and that `tested_sha` is updated on entering `awaiting_verified`, but the plan never specifies what happens to the wipChain record after a SUCCESSFUL submit — whether phase transitions to `idle`, whether the record is cleared/reset, and whether the next FIFO selection cycle begins immediately or on the next polling tick. This is the most critical state transition in the loop.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC5 — wipChain schema
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC5: 'On successful submit: phase → idle; all wipChain fields except phase are cleared; the selection cycle restarts on the next poll interval.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add at least one concrete proving command or test reference per AC (e.g. a pytest test name/path, a `curl` sequence against a Gerrit stub, or a named integration scenario) so that done-ness is verifiable without round-tripping to the author.
findings.finding: The plan contains zero proving commands or described tests for any of its seven acceptance criteria — no executor can verify done-ness without inventing the entire test strategy.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria (all bullets)

findings.finding: No acceptance criterion has an associated proving command or verification step — the plan defines six behavioral ACs but provides zero concrete commands (unit test invocation, integration test, API query, log assertion) that an executor or reviewer can run to confirm each criterion is met.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria (all six ACs)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the concrete re-drive attempt limit (e.g., N=3) and the observable exit behavior at exhaustion in the TOCTOU guard AC, so the goal is verifiably testable.
findings.finding: The re-drive loop bound ('bounded re-drive attempts') has no observable proof of termination behavior: the AC specifies 'bounded' but names no limit and defines no observable outcome at exhaustion, making this non-deferred goal unverifiable.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — FFO TOCTOU guard + re-drive bullet
findings.suggested_fix: Amend the AC: '…looping up to N=3 re-drive attempts; if the Nth attempt also finds main has advanced, abandon the wipChain (reset to idle, log a warning) and select the next eligible change.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the RETIRE path for wipChain: specify the phase transition on successful submit (→ idle/cleared), the behavior at re-drive exhaustion (abandon + idle/alert), and how the next change is selected after either outcome.
findings.finding: The wipChain RETIRE/reset path is unspecified: the plan never states what happens to wipChain after a successful submit (does it reset to idle? get cleared?), nor what happens when bounded re-drive attempts are exhausted — the in-memory record may effectively leak (remain stuck in a terminal phase with no consumer able to proceed).
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — wipChain schema bullet and FFO TOCTOU guard bullet
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC: 'On successful submit, wipChain resets to `{phase: idle}` and the loop immediately re-runs selection. On re-drive exhaustion (define N, e.g. 3), wipChain is cleared, the change is removed from consideration (optionally a `Autosubmit -1` or comment is cast — deferred), and selection restarts.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a testing plan (at minimum: unit tests for rebase-selection branching, fresh-Verified detection, and the TOCTOU re-drive loop; at least one failure-path test for re-drive bound exhaustion and stale-SHA detection) before closing this ticket.
findings.finding: No testing plan exists for the substantial new state-machine logic introduced (wipChain phases, rebase-selection branching, fresh-Verified detection, TOCTOU guard, bounded re-drive loop). The plan contains zero mention of tests, test strategy, or test ACs, leaving the entire new behavior unvalidated.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria (all ACs) and Scope section
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Testing' section to the plan with: (1) unit tests for the three-way rebase-selection rule using mocked RelatedChanges responses; (2) unit tests for fresh-Verified detection (carried-vote vs. new-patchset-vote); (3) state-machine tests cycling through all phase transitions; (4) a failure-path test for re-drive bound exhaustion (expect idle/error phase); (5) a TOCTOU test where main advances between submit-check and submit, verifying re-drive is triggered rather than stale submit.

findings.finding: The plan introduces new live Gerrit API boundaries (POST /rebase, POST /rebase:chain, POST /submit, Verified vote polling) but specifies no testing strategy at all — not even a thin acceptance test. With no stated live/end-to-end criterion, the story can be closed green with offline-mock-only tests that never exercise the actual Gerrit submit path, satisfying all ACs while leaving the live integration path completely untested.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria (all ACs) / Scope section — testing approach absent throughout
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC such as: 'A smoke/integration test against a test Gerrit instance (or replay fixture capturing real Gerrit HTTP responses) executes the full select→rebase→await-Verified→submit loop and asserts the submitted SHA matches the rebased patchset SHA.' If live testing is deferred, name the child ticket explicitly.

findings.finding: The plan introduces substantial new branching logic (rebase-selection rule, Verified-freshness check, TOCTOU guard, FIFO ordering) but contains zero testing plan — no unit tests, integration tests, or deferred test child ticket — leaving all failure paths (SHA mismatch, stale vote, main advancing mid-flight, chain vs. single-change detection) completely unspecified for coverage.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — all ACs; Scope section

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Co-locate with the `wipChain` schema AC the specific Gerrit API call and response field used to confirm a fresh Verified +1 (e.g., 'poll `GET /changes/{id}/revisions/{tested_sha}/votes` and confirm a `Verified` entry with value=1 exists on the post-rebase SHA').
findings.finding: The 'fresh Verified +1' detection protocol (how the service distinguishes a genuinely new vote from a TRIVIAL_REBASE-carried vote) is stated in one AC bullet but is not co-located with the `wipChain` schema that must encode its result — and the detection mechanism itself (which Gerrit API fields to inspect) is never specified.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — fresh Verified bullet ('defined as a Verified +1 on the exact rebased patchset SHA (not a copied/carried vote from a prior patchset)')
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the fresh-Verified AC: 'Detect freshness by querying `GET /changes/{id}/revisions/{tested_sha}/votes` — a vote present on this revision endpoint is by definition patchset-scoped and cannot be a carried vote from a prior patchset. Store the confirmed SHA in `wipChain.verified_at`; assert `verified_at == tested_sha` before proceeding to submitting.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the type and semantics of `wipChain.verified_at` in the schema AC (e.g., `verified_at: str | None` storing the patchset commit SHA for which Verified +1 was observed, or a patchset number, or a vote event ID), and specify which Gerrit API field populates it.
findings.finding: The `wipChain.verified_at` field's value vocabulary (type and semantics) is never defined — it is a schema field an executor must populate and read to enforce the 'fresh Verified' invariant, yet its value type is unspecified.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — `wipChain` schema bullet (`verified_at` field)
findings.suggested_fix: Change schema to `verified_at: str | None` defined as 'the commit SHA of the patchset carrying the fresh Verified +1 (must equal `tested_sha` before submit is allowed)'; populate from the Gerrit vote's `currentPatchSet.revision` field.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reconcile the Scope section with AC4: either explicitly carve re-drive/TOCTOU retry out of 'failure paths' (e.g., 'happy path includes TOCTOU re-drive; external error handling is out of scope') or move the re-drive AC to the failure-paths story.
findings.finding: The 'Scope' section explicitly excludes failure paths ('Failure paths... are separate stories'), yet AC item 4 specifies a detailed re-drive retry loop — 'reset its phase to rebasing, rebase-to-new-tip → await fresh Verified → re-check, looping (bounded re-drive attempts)' — which is a non-trivial failure/recovery path, not a happy-path concern. An implementer reading Scope would omit re-drive; reading AC4 they must implement it.
findings.location: Scope section vs. AC item 4 (FFO TOCTOU guard + re-drive)
findings.suggested_fix: Add a parenthetical to Scope: 'Happy-path loop + serialization + identity/attribution + FFO TOCTOU re-drive (bounded). External error handling, deploy, and the agent-facing command are separate stories.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the polling interval and maximum wait timeout for `awaiting_verified` (e.g. poll every 30 s, timeout after 2 h) and the action on timeout (e.g. drop wipChain, reset to idle).
findings.finding: No polling interval or maximum wait duration is stated for the 'await fresh Verified +1' phase, leaving the executor to choose an arbitrary polling cadence and timeout.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — fresh Verified AC: 'Awaits a fresh Verified +1'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the Verified AC: 'Poll `GET /changes/{id}?o=DETAILED_LABELS` every 30 s; if no fresh Verified +1 within 2 h, abandon wipChain (reset to `idle`) and log structured WARNING — failure-path handling is a separate story but the timeout bound is required for this loop to terminate.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the re-drive attempt bound as a named, configurable parameter (e.g., `MAX_REDRIVE_ATTEMPTS`, settable via environment variable or config file) and document its default value and tuning guidance in the service config spec.
findings.finding: The 'bounded re-drive attempts' threshold in the TOCTOU guard is operationally sensitive (too low → legitimate chains fail; too high → runaway loops) but is neither specified nor marked as configurable in the plan.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — FFO TOCTOU guard + re-drive AC
findings.suggested_fix: Define `MAX_REDRIVE_ATTEMPTS = int(os.getenv('AUTOLANDER_MAX_REDRIVE', '5'))` and note the default in the design doc / README alongside the expected re-drive rate under typical main-branch velocity.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a rebase-selection rule for **mixed** relation chains (chains containing both merge and non-merge member changes): specify whether the service should (a) detect and reject such chains as unsupported, (b) apply per-member rebase calls sequentially, or (c) split the chain at merge-commit boundaries — and update AC2 accordingly.
findings.finding: The rebase-selection rule (AC2) specifies behavior for pure cases (all-non-merge chain, single non-merge, merge-at-tip) but is silent on a mixed relation chain containing BOTH merge and non-merge member changes. If a wipChain has an interior merge commit flanked by non-merge commits, neither `rebase:chain` (excluded for any merge member) nor a single `POST /changes/{id}/rebase` (designed for one change) is clearly applicable, and calling `rebase:chain` on a chain with an embedded merge commit is likely rejected by Gerrit's API — leaving the service with no defined code path.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC2 — Rebase-selection rule

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a `rebase_attempt_count` (or equivalent) field to the wipChain schema in AC5, specify its maximum value, and state what phase the service transitions to when that maximum is exceeded (even if the failure disposition is deferred to a later story).
findings.finding: The 'bounded re-drive attempts' constraint in AC4 is named but its bound is unquantified, and the wipChain schema (AC5) includes no retry counter or attempt-tracking field. Without a counter in the schema, the 'bounded' invariant cannot be enforced — there is no state to check against the bound.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC4 (re-drive bound) and AC5 (wipChain schema)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a co-located state-transition table to the `wipChain` schema AC specifying all legal arcs (e.g., idle→selecting, selecting→rebasing, rebasing→awaiting_verified, awaiting_verified→submitting, submitting→idle, submitting→rebasing for re-drive) and the event/condition that triggers each.
findings.finding: The `wipChain` schema defines `phase ∈ {selecting, rebasing, awaiting_verified, submitting, idle}` but no state-transition table or protocol is co-located with the schema AC — an executor must invent valid transitions (e.g., idle→selecting trigger, submitting→idle on success, whether submitting→rebasing is a direct arc).
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — `wipChain` schema bullet (`phase ∈ {selecting, rebasing, awaiting_verified, submitting, idle}`)
findings.suggested_fix: Extend the `wipChain` schema AC with a transition table: `idle -[front chain found]→ selecting → rebasing → awaiting_verified → submitting -[success]→ idle; submitting -[TOCTOU fail, attempts < N]→ rebasing; awaiting_verified -[change abandoned/timeout]→ idle (deferred to S3).`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `attempt_count: int` (or equivalent) to the `wipChain` schema, specify the numeric re-drive bound (e.g., `MAX_REDRIVES = 3`), and define the fallback action when the bound is exceeded (e.g., transition to a new `failed` phase, log, clear wipChain to idle).
findings.finding: The TOCTOU guard specifies 'bounded re-drive attempts' but neither defines the bound nor includes an attempt counter in the `wipChain` schema — a counter with ambiguous placement and an unspecified fallback when the bound is exceeded.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — FFO TOCTOU guard bullet ('looping (bounded re-drive attempts)')
findings.suggested_fix: Add to wipChain schema: `{change_id, chain_member_ids[], tested_sha, verified_at, phase, rebase_attempt: int}`. Specify `MAX_REBASE_ATTEMPTS = 3`. On `rebase_attempt >= MAX_REBASE_ATTEMPTS`: emit structured log, clear wipChain to `idle`, allow the next FIFO candidate to be selected.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a minimal error-boundary AC: specify that any Gerrit API error during the core loop transitions `wipChain` to a defined error/idle state (releasing the serializer lock) and surfaces the error, even if full retry/backoff logic is deferred to a later story.
findings.finding: The plan explicitly defers all failure paths to a separate story but does not define even the minimal error boundary for the core loop: what happens when a Gerrit API call (POST /rebase, POST /rebase:chain, POST /submit) returns a non-2xx response (e.g., rebase conflict, 409, 503). Without at least specifying that the loop must transition wipChain to a safe/released state on any API error, the core loop has no safe exit and may stall or hold wipChain indefinitely.
findings.location: Scope section + AC: 'Ancestor-atomic submit' + 'Failure paths... are separate stories'
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC: 'On any non-retryable Gerrit API error (rebase conflict, unexpected non-2xx), the loop MUST transition wipChain.phase to idle, remove the Autosubmit+1 vote (or flag the change), and emit a structured log event — deferring retry/alerting to the failure-paths story but never leaving wipChain stuck.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm whether the autolander service extends `src/rebar/review_bot/gerrit_client.GerritClient` with the new endpoints (rebase, related-changes, label-search, submit) or introduces a standalone Gerrit HTTP client in its own package — either is valid, but the plan should state the choice to avoid NIH duplication of auth/XSSI/transport boilerplate.
findings.finding: The plan specifies a new 'project service' that must make Gerrit REST calls (query changes by label, GET related-changes, POST rebase, POST rebase:chain, POST submit, GET votes) but is silent on whether it reuses or rebuilds the existing production GerritClient HTTP transport in src/rebar/review_bot/gerrit_client.py. That client already handles XSSI stripping, Basic-auth construction, urllib transport, and GerritError wrapping — duplicating this for the new service is NIH, but the plan's explicit 'NOT src/rebar core' boundary makes the relationship ambiguous.
findings.location: What section / 'A project service (Python, project infra — NOT src/rebar core)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a sentence to the 'What' or 'Scope' section: e.g. 'The service adds the rebase/submit/related-changes endpoints to a new `autolander.gerrit_client` module that copies the stdlib-urllib + XSSI-strip + Basic-auth pattern from `review_bot/gerrit_client.py` (same codebase convention, separate package).' or 'extends `review_bot.gerrit_client.GerritClient` with the new endpoint methods.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Document the crash-recovery posture for wipChain: either specify that in-memory state is intentionally ephemeral (service re-selects from scratch on restart, idempotency of rebase handled by Gerrit) or add a persistence/reconstruction AC.
findings.finding: wipChain CREATE lifecycle (initialization) is unspecified: the plan does not state whether wipChain starts as `idle` at service boot, is persisted across restarts, or is reconstructed — meaning a crash mid-flight loses all state with no defined recovery.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — wipChain schema bullet; Scope section
findings.suggested_fix: Add a note: 'wipChain is ephemeral; on service restart it initializes to idle and re-runs selection. Gerrit's rebase endpoint is idempotent on a no-op rebase (already at tip), so re-selecting an in-flight change is safe. The Verified re-await ensures no stale SHA is submitted.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an ADR (or CLAUDE.md / design-doc section) capturing the serial auto-lander's core architectural decisions: the `wipChain` state-machine schema and phase transitions, the three-branch rebase-selection rule (chain / single / merge-first-parent), the FIFO-by-vote-timestamp ordering contract, and the re-drive loop rationale.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a novel project service with a `wipChain` state machine, FIFO-by-vote-timestamp ordering, merge-vs-chain rebase discrimination, and a re-drive loop — none of which are captured in an ADR or design doc, despite being non-trivial architectural decisions.
findings.location: ## What, ## Acceptance Criteria (wipChain schema AC, rebase-selection rule AC)
findings.suggested_fix: Create `docs/adr/NNNN-serial-autolander-core-loop.md` covering: (1) why a single `wipChain` / serial instance, (2) `wipChain` schema and phase FSM diagram, (3) rebase-selection decision tree with the merge-change exception rationale, (4) FIFO ordering key, (5) re-drive loop bounds and exit conditions.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a targeted empirical probe (against a Gerrit sandbox or via fixture replay of real API responses) to validate: (a) that `RelatedChanges` responses contain sufficient fields to identify merge commits, and (b) that 'fresh vs. carried' `Verified` vote detection by patchset SHA is reliable given Gerrit's `TRIVIAL_REBASE`/`MERGE_FIRST_PARENT_UPDATE` copy semantics — before committing to these API contracts in the full build.
findings.finding: Two Gerrit API behaviors central to correctness are asserted without empirical validation: (a) that `GET /changes/{id}/revisions/current/related` reliably distinguishes merge commits from regular commits within a chain (enabling the merge-vs-chain rebase branch), and (b) that a 'fresh' `Verified +1` can be distinguished from a carried/copied vote by matching the exact rebased patchset SHA via the Gerrit API.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — rebase-selection rule AC, awaiting_verified AC

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the restart-recovery contract for `wipChain`: either persist state (e.g., to a file or Gerrit change metadata) so the loop can resume, OR explicitly document that restart always resets to `idle` and any in-flight chain must be requeued (losing in-progress CI), with an AC that the loop re-selects and re-drives correctly from scratch after restart.
findings.finding: The `wipChain` state is held in-memory only. If the process restarts (crash, deploy, OOM kill), the in-flight chain is left mid-rebase with no recovery path: the rebased patchset exists in Gerrit but the loop has no record of it, and the next iteration may select and re-rebase the same chain, or worse, miss that a Verified vote was already earned on the rebased SHA.
findings.location: AC: '`wipChain` schema: a single in-memory record'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC specifying a maximum wait timeout for the `awaiting_verified` phase (e.g., configurable, default 2 hours); on timeout, transition `wipChain` to idle and surface an error/alert so operators can investigate.
findings.finding: The plan specifies no timeout for the 'awaiting_verified' phase. If CI never posts a Verified +1 (CI system down, job lost, vote suppressed), the loop waits indefinitely, holding wipChain and blocking all subsequent submissions.
findings.location: AC: 'Awaits a fresh Verified +1 — defined as a Verified +1 on the exact rebased patchset SHA'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an observability AC: the loop MUST emit structured log events on each phase transition (`selecting→rebasing`, `rebasing→awaiting_verified`, etc.) and on any error, with at minimum the `change_id`, `phase`, and outcome — even if dashboards/alerts are deferred to a later story.
findings.finding: The plan contains no observability specification for the loop's state transitions, Gerrit API calls, or failure events. There are no mentions of structured logs, metrics (e.g., queue depth, rebase latency, submit success rate), or alerts.
findings.location: Entire plan — no observability section or AC

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a rollout AC (even if deploy details are deferred): the loop MUST support a runtime kill-switch (e.g., env var, config flag, or Gerrit-based enable flag) that halts selection without process restart, so the service can be safely deactivated if it misbehaves in production.
findings.finding: The plan defers deploy entirely to a separate story but provides no rollout or rollback intent (feature flag, canary, kill-switch). Since this is a new always-on long-running service that takes over submit decisions previously made by humans, a silent 100%-traffic activation with no stated rollback mechanism is a rollout risk.
findings.location: Scope: 'deploy... [is a] separate story'
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC: 'The loop reads a `AUTOLANDER_ENABLED` flag (env var or config) at the top of each select iteration; when false, it skips selection and logs a warning — enabling instant, no-redeploy deactivation.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add at least one AC or validation note that provides a concrete mechanism to verify the race-elimination goal (e.g., a dogfooding note, a live integration smoke test, or an operational criterion such as 'no concurrent rebase-to-tip submissions observed under a two-agent test scenario').
findings.finding: No AC carries a concrete validation mechanism (before/after workflow comparison, operational metric, or dogfooding criterion) that would let a reviewer verify the value claim ('parallel agent sessions stop hand-rolling it and racing') has actually been achieved.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria section; Why section

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a spike or cite an existing evidence doc verifying that `on_behalf_of_uploader=true` on `POST /changes/{id}/rebase` preserves `Signed-off-by` and `rebar-ticket` trailers in the rebar Gerrit instance (check DCO enforcement compatibility), and record the finding in a runbook or ADR before implementing.
findings.finding: The plan asserts 'on_behalf_of_uploader=true preserves author/DCO Signed-off-by/rebar-ticket trailer' — an empirically-checkable claim about Gerrit API behavior — but no in-repo documentation, runbook, test, or evidence file corroborates that this parameter has been verified to preserve these specific trailers in this project's Gerrit instance.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — Rebase-selection rule: 'on_behalf_of_uploader=true preserves author/DCO Signed-off-by/rebar-ticket trailer'
findings.suggested_fix: Create a one-off test change with a DCO Signed-off-by and rebar-ticket trailer, rebase it via the API with on_behalf_of_uploader=true, and inspect the resulting patchset's commit message. Record the outcome in docs/autolander-evidence.md and reference it from this plan.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Cite or produce an evidence doc (e.g., a live Gerrit API call log or Gerrit REST API reference excerpt) confirming that `POST /changes/{id}/rebase` on a `--no-ff` merge change produces a `MERGE_FIRST_PARENT_UPDATE` patchset and that `POST /rebase:chain` rejects or misbehaves on chains containing merge members.
findings.finding: The plan asserts as a design rule that a '--no-ff merge change is ALWAYS rebased with POST /changes/{id}/rebase… NEVER /rebase:chain, even when it sits at a chain tip' — an empirically-checkable claim about Gerrit API behavior — but no in-repo documentation, runbook, ADR, or evidence file corroborates that this endpoint discrimination has been tested or observed to be correct in this Gerrit instance.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — Rebase-selection rule: 'A --no-ff merge change is ALWAYS rebased with POST /changes/{id}/rebase (first-parent-only → MERGE_FIRST_PARENT_UPDATE), NEVER /rebase:chain'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate `infra/gerrit/setup-project.sh` as an S2 edit target and add a step that provisions `autolander-bot` into the `Contributors` group (parallel to the existing `CONTRIBUTOR_MEMBERS` convergence block), so the service account holds the `submit` permission required by AC4.
findings.finding: The `autolander-bot` service account is not enumerated as a member of the `Contributors` group, yet `infra/gerrit/project.config` restricts `POST /submit` to `submit = group Contributors`. The plan never names `infra/gerrit/setup-project.sh` as an edit target for S2, leaving `autolander-bot` unable to land any change at runtime.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC4 ('Ancestor-atomic submit of the exact tested SHA'); infra/gerrit/project.config line 63 (`submit = group Contributors`); infra/gerrit/setup-project.sh `want=` dict (line 123)
findings.suggested_fix: Add `AUTOLANDER_BOT_USER="autolander-bot"` and a `gerrit set-members Contributors --add "$AUTOLANDER_BOT_USER"` block in `setup-project.sh` (mirroring the existing `CONTRIBUTOR_MEMBERS` loop), and list `infra/gerrit/setup-project.sh` explicitly in the plan's edit-target enumeration.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name the concrete file (e.g. a new `infra/gerrit/setup-autolander-bot.sh` or an extension of `service-user.sh`) that provisions the `autolander-bot` Gerrit service account and its credential, and list it as an explicit edit target in S2's scope.
findings.finding: No provisioning script or identity-creation step for `autolander-bot` is named in S2, yet the service identity must exist and hold the `Autosubmit Voters` group membership (from S1) AND a Gerrit HTTP/SSH credential before S2's loop can authenticate. S2 names no file that creates or configures this account, and the only existing provisioning scripts (`service-user.sh`, `setup-ci-service-account.sh`) cover the review-bot and CI voter respectively.
findings.location: What section ('identity/attribution'); Dependencies section; infra/gerrit/service-user.sh; infra/gerrit/setup-ci-service-account.sh

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a brief positive rationale to the 'What' or a dedicated 'Approach' section explaining why the polling-loop + single in-memory wipChain model was chosen (e.g., simplicity, alignment with single-instance serialization requirement, no new event-delivery infrastructure, restartability).
findings.finding: The plan states no positive rationale for the chosen architecture (polling loop + single in-memory wipChain + single-instance service). The 'Why' section explains the problem and 'What' describes the mechanism, but there is no stated reasoning for why this design — vs. alternatives such as a webhook/event-driven state machine or a lightweight durable queue — was selected. The absence of positive rationale is a criterion requirement.
findings.location: Why and What sections; no rationale section present
findings.suggested_fix: Add: 'Chosen approach rationale: a simple polling loop with a single in-memory wipChain is sufficient at this scale (low landing frequency), requires no new event-delivery infrastructure, and the single-instance model provides serialization by construction. Durability of wipChain state is intentionally deferred — a crash mid-rebase is recoverable by re-selecting the front change on restart.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the unverified capability assertion: run a spike against the live Gerrit instance (or a test instance) that exercises `POST /changes/{id}/rebase` and `POST /rebase:chain` with `on_behalf_of_uploader=true` and asserts that the resulting patchset's commit message retains all three: original author line, `Signed-off-by` trailer, and `rebar-ticket` trailer — documenting the result in an ADR or evidence note before committing to this design.
findings.finding: The plan asserts `on_behalf_of_uploader=true` preserves author identity, DCO `Signed-off-by`, and `rebar-ticket` trailers through a Gerrit API-driven rebase (both `/rebase` and `/rebase:chain`), but this specific capability combination is unverified anywhere in the codebase — no ADR, no spike, no runbook, no evidence doc confirms Gerrit's rebase API actually carries commit-message trailers intact when operating on behalf of the uploader.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — Rebase-selection rule: 'on_behalf_of_uploader=true preserves author/DCO Signed-off-by/rebar-ticket trailer'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a one-shot probe script (e.g., `infra/gerrit/probe-rebase-on-behalf-of.sh`) that: (1) creates a test change with a known author, Signed-off-by, and rebar-ticket trailer; (2) calls POST /rebase with on_behalf_of_uploader=true; (3) fetches the resulting patchset commit message and asserts all three trailers are present. Record the output as `docs/88ab-autolander-on-behalf-of-evidence.md` (following the pattern of `docs/88ab-feature-branch-evidence.md`) and reference it from the AC.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify `POST /changes/{id}/rebase:chain` is live and functional on the rebar Gerrit 3.14.1 instance (probe with a test chain change; record the request/response schema in a spike doc or evidence file) before implementing the chain-rebase path.
findings.finding: POST /changes/{id}/rebase:chain is named as the core chain-rebase endpoint but has zero usage, no spike, and no evidence of verification anywhere in the codebase or project docs; it is an entirely new Gerrit REST surface for this project.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — Rebase-selection rule: 'a chain of >1 non-merge member rebases with POST /rebase:chain'
findings.suggested_fix: Create a two-change test chain, call POST /a/changes/{id}/rebase:chain with an authenticated request (Basic auth, bot credentials), observe the HTTP status and response body, and record the verified schema in docs/88ab-feature-branch-evidence.md or a new autolander-api-evidence.md.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Probe `GET /changes/{id}/revisions/current/related` on the live Gerrit 3.14.1 instance against both a single change and a multi-change relation chain; record the response schema (especially the `changes[]` field and how to distinguish chain members) in a verification artifact before implementing chain detection.
findings.finding: GET /changes/{id}/revisions/current/related is named as the chain-detection endpoint but is entirely new to this project's integration surface, with no codebase usage or verification evidence.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — Rebase-selection rule: 'detect a relation chain via GET /changes/{id}/revisions/current/related (the RelatedChanges endpoint)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a spike to verify that (a) the `autolander-bot` Gerrit account has (or can be granted) the `forgeAuthor`/`forgeCommitter` global capability needed for `on_behalf_of_uploader=true` rebases, and (b) that `Signed-off-by` and `rebar-ticket` trailers are intact on the resulting patchset — record required ACL additions in the plan before implementing.
findings.finding: The plan asserts `on_behalf_of_uploader=true` preserves author identity, DCO `Signed-off-by`, and `rebar-ticket` trailers through both /rebase and /rebase:chain calls, but this Gerrit capability combination is Unverified anywhere in the codebase, and Gerrit's `on_behalf_of_uploader` rebase API requires the bot account to hold a forge-identity global capability (forgeAuthor or equivalent) that is not mentioned in the plan's ACL specification.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — Rebase-selection rule: 'on_behalf_of_uploader=true preserves author/DCO Signed-off-by/rebar-ticket trailer'
findings.suggested_fix: In the S1 (Autosubmit label) story or a new ACL story, add a `[capability] forgeAuthor = group Autosubmit Voters` (or equivalent `rebaser` permission) block to project.config for the autolander-bot; then probe on_behalf_of_uploader with a test change carrying both trailers and confirm preservation in a spike doc.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State explicitly which Gerrit groups `autolander-bot` must belong to (at minimum: `Autosubmit Voters` for label votes, `Contributors` for Submit, and any group needed for rebase/forge-identity), and add the corresponding `setup-project.sh` membership-convergence steps to the plan's deliverables.
findings.finding: The plan specifies no auth preconditions beyond the Autosubmit vote ACL for the autolander-bot account — the additional Gerrit permissions the bot needs to call POST /submit, POST /rebase, and POST /rebase:chain (specifically: Submit permission on refs/heads/*, rebase capability, and forge-identity capability) are not stated.
findings.location: Scope section and Acceptance Criteria — 'ancestor-atomic submit of the exact tested SHA'
findings.suggested_fix: Add autolander-bot to the CONTRIBUTOR_MEMBERS list in setup-project.sh (paralleling RebarBotNava) and document the full required group membership in the plan or a new ACL story.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `autolander-bot`'s Gerrit REST credential to the secret lifecycle: create an SSM SecureString slot (e.g., `/rebar/prod/autolander-bot-token`) in `ssm.tf`, extend (or create a sibling of) `service-user.sh` to provision the account + rotate the token, and add the fetch mapping to `fetch-secrets.sh` so the service receives it at runtime without plaintext exposure.
findings.finding: No credential provisioning or secret lifecycle is specified for `autolander-bot`'s Gerrit REST token — there is no SSM parameter slot, no provisioning script reference, and no fetch-secrets.sh injection path declared.
findings.location: Scope section / Dependencies — secret lifecycle for autolander-bot credential absent from plan; ssm.tf rebar_secret_params has no autolander-bot entry

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Declare the minimum submit ACL grant for `autolander-bot` in the plan (e.g., a dedicated `submit = group Autosubmit Voters` line in `project.config` under `[access "refs/heads/*"]`, scoped narrowly to that group), and confirm `exclusiveGroupPermissions = submit` covers the new line — avoiding placement in the overly-broad `Contributors` or `Administrators` groups.
findings.finding: The plan does not specify how `autolander-bot` will hold the `submit` permission required to call `POST /submit`; the existing `project.config` restricts submit to `Contributors` + `Administrators` with `exclusiveGroupPermissions`, so the bot will receive 403 unless an explicit, least-privilege grant is added.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'Ancestor-atomic submit of the exact tested SHA' / infra/gerrit/project.config [access "refs/heads/*"] submit ACL
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the plan's Scope or a new Security section: 'project.config gains a scoped `submit = group Autosubmit Voters` under `[access "refs/heads/*"]` so autolander-bot can call POST /submit without being added to Contributors or Administrators; no other ACL changes.'

findings.finding: The plan asserts 'on_behalf_of_uploader=true preserves author/DCO Signed-off-by/rebar-ticket trailer' as a parameter of POST /changes/{id}/rebase and POST /rebase:chain, but the string 'on_behalf_of_uploader' appears nowhere in the codebase (src/, docs/, infra/). The Gerrit REST API has an 'on_behalf_of' parameter for review votes, not for rebase — if this field name is wrong or inapplicable to the rebase endpoint, the DCO Signed-off-by preservation guarantee silently fails, and every rebased commit will carry the bot's identity instead of the original author's.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — Rebase-selection rule (second AC bullet): 'on_behalf_of_uploader=true preserves author/DCO Signed-off-by/rebar-ticket trailer'

findings.finding: POST /changes/{id}/rebase:chain with on_behalf_of_uploader is a load-bearing endpoint the plan depends on for chain rebase + DCO/author preservation, but it has zero prior use in the codebase, no spike or probe is planned, and neither the endpoint's availability nor its on_behalf_of_uploader trailer-propagation behavior across all chain members is empirically grounded for Gerrit 3.14.1.
findings.location: AC2 — 'if the change heads a relation chain with >1 member … use `POST /rebase:chain`; … `on_behalf_of_uploader=true` preserves author/DCO `Signed-off-by`/`rebar-ticket` trailer'
findings.suggested_fix: Create `infra/spikes/rebase_chain_probe.py` that: (1) creates two stacked test changes with DCO trailers, (2) calls POST /a/changes/{id}/rebase:chain with on_behalf_of_uploader=true, (3) fetches the resulting patchset commit messages and asserts Signed-off-by and rebar-ticket are present. Record the response schema (single ChangeInfo vs list) to fix AC5's tested_sha update logic.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the Gerrit auth mechanism to use (e.g. 'HTTP password for autolander-bot service account, loaded from env var `GERRIT_HTTP_PASSWORD`') so the executor does not have to choose.
findings.finding: The Gerrit API authentication mechanism is not stated, leaving the executor to choose between HTTP basic auth, OAuth tokens, service account cookies, or HTTP passwords — a non-trivial implementation decision affecting the entire API call surface.
findings.location: What / Scope sections — all Gerrit API interactions
findings.suggested_fix: Add a Constraints section: 'Authenticate to Gerrit as `autolander-bot` using HTTP password auth (`Authorization: Basic base64(user:password)`); credentials sourced from env var `AUTOLANDER_GERRIT_PASSWORD`.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add the FIFO ordering rule (keyed on Autosubmit vote timestamp, oldest-first) to the 'What' section so the selection algorithm is described in the body, not only in the AC.
findings.finding: AC item 1 specifies FIFO ordering 'keyed on the Autosubmit vote's timestamp (oldest-voted-submittable-first)' but the 'What' section only says 'for the front Autosubmit+submittable change/chain' without describing this ordering rule. The selection algorithm is a deliverable mentioned only in the AC, leaving it orphaned from the body description.
findings.location: AC item 1 vs. 'What' section
findings.suggested_fix: Append to the 'What' section: 'Selection is FIFO by the Autosubmit vote timestamp — the chain whose Autosubmit vote was cast earliest among all currently submittable candidates is processed first.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Declare the authentication mechanism for `autolander-bot`'s Gerrit REST calls (e.g., HTTP Basic with a per-bot token stored in SSM `/rebar/prod/autolander-bot-token` following the `service-user.sh` / `gerrit-bot-token` pattern) in the plan's Scope or a new Security section.
findings.finding: The plan does not declare how 'autolander-bot' authenticates to the Gerrit REST API (HTTP Basic token, SSH, OAuth) — the access posture of the outbound boundary-crossing path is undeclared.
findings.location: What section / Acceptance Criteria (identity/attribution noted in Scope; no auth mechanism stated anywhere in the plan)
findings.suggested_fix: Add a sentence to the Scope section: 'autolander-bot authenticates to the Gerrit REST API via HTTP Basic using a dedicated token provisioned by a new service-user.sh invocation and stored in SSM /rebar/prod/autolander-bot-token (following the rebar-review-bot pattern); the token is fetched at runtime via fetch-secrets.sh and never written to disk or logs.'

findings.finding: AC2's chain-detection mechanism conflates two distinct Gerrit APIs. The plan parenthetically says 'detected via `GET /changes/{id}?o=CURRENT_REVISION` + the related-changes / `SUBMITTED_TOGETHER` set' without specifying whether chain detection uses the `?o=SUBMITTED_TOGETHER` query option on the changes list endpoint or the separate `GET /changes/{id}/related` endpoint. `SUBMITTED_TOGETHER` does not appear anywhere in the codebase; `related-changes` also does not appear. These are different APIs with different response shapes and chain-membership semantics.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC2 (Rebase-selection rule)

findings.finding: The plan's rebase-selection rule cites 'GET /changes/{id}?o=CURRENT_REVISION + the related-changes / SUBMITTED_TOGETHER set' to detect relation-chain membership, but SUBMITTED_TOGETHER does not appear anywhere in the codebase (src/, docs/, infra/). If this is a Gerrit change-detail option (like CURRENT_REVISION), its availability on the running Gerrit version is unverifiable; if the option name or the API path is wrong, the chain-detection logic — and the entire rebase-selection branch — will silently fail to detect multi-member chains.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — Rebase-selection rule (second AC bullet)
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'the related-changes / SUBMITTED_TOGETHER set' with a verified endpoint: `GET /changes/{id}/related` returns a `changes[]` array with `_change_number`, `status`, and `commit` fields; use that to enumerate chain members. Confirm against the live Gerrit 3.14 API docs and add a note to the AC citing the endpoint.

findings.finding: The `phase` field in the wipChain schema (AC5) is named but its value vocabulary is never defined anywhere in the plan.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC5 (`wipChain` schema)
findings.suggested_fix: Add a `phase` enum table to AC5, e.g.: `phase ∈ {selecting, rebasing, awaiting_verified, submitting}`; annotate which AC drives each transition (AC2→rebasing, AC3→awaiting_verified, AC4→submitting or back to rebasing).

findings.finding: AC4's TOCTOU guard mandates 're-drive rebase' on SHA mismatch or `main` advancement, but the decision protocol for what 're-drive' means — which `phase` to reset to, what to do with the existing `wipChain` record — is not co-located with the guard rule or the AC5 schema.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC4 (FFO TOCTOU guard) and AC5 (`wipChain` schema)

findings.finding: The wipChain schema AC includes a `phase` field but provides no enumerated values or transition rules for it, leaving a deliverable sub-element with no covering criterion.
findings.location: AC: '`wipChain` schema' bullet

findings.finding: The plan names `SUBMITTED_TOGETHER` as a query option (via `GET /changes/{id}?o=CURRENT_REVISION` + the `SUBMITTED_TOGETHER` set) for chain detection, but this option string has zero prior use in the codebase and its availability as a `?o=` option in Gerrit 3.14.1 is unverified.
findings.location: AC2 — 'detected via `GET /changes/{id}?o=CURRENT_REVISION` + the related-changes / `SUBMITTED_TOGETHER` set'
findings.suggested_fix: In the spike, also call GET /changes/{id}/related and GET /changes/{id}?o=SUBMITTED_TOGETHER (if available) on a known chain, and record which correctly identifies all chain members. If SUBMITTED_TOGETHER is topic-scoped (not chain-scoped), replace it with GET /changes/{id}/related in AC2.

findings.finding: The AC2 rule 'A merge commit that is *also* a chain tip is treated as a chain → use POST /rebase:chain' describes a mechanically broken operation: Gerrit's rebase:chain API is designed for relation chains of ordinary (non-merge) commits and cannot correctly rebase a --no-ff merge commit as a chain head. Applying it to a merge-commit chain tip would strip the merge object's second parent, converting it to an ordinary commit and destroying the feature-branch topology that ADR-0025 and ADR-0040 depend on. The correct operation for a merge commit at a chain tip is a client-side re-merge producing a MERGE_FIRST_PARENT_UPDATE patchset, not a server-side rebase:chain call.
findings.location: AC2 — Rebase-selection rule: 'A merge commit that is *also* a chain tip is treated as a chain'
findings.suggested_fix: Revise AC2 to add a third case: if the change is a --no-ff merge commit (regardless of chain membership), the autolander performs a client-side re-merge onto the current main tip and pushes a new patchset for review, producing MERGE_FIRST_PARENT_UPDATE. Reserve POST /rebase:chain for relation chains of ordinary (non-merge) commits only, and POST /changes/{id}/rebase for single ordinary changes.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
